changelog: v5.0.0: The snapshot-sensor release. FAST now reads GPU clocks, temps, power, fan speed, PCIe link state, throttle flags, CPU frequency, thermal zones, and mouse polling directly from the driver libraries NVIDIA and AMD ship, plus built-in Windows APIs -- no kernel driver, no background polling, zero sensor traffic when idle or behind a game. Home page rebuilt around dual pipelines: FPS Pipeline (4 stages flagging config issues per stage) and Latency Pipeline (5 stages with NVIDIA-style brackets showing real ms contributions; 1000 Hz mouse = 1 ms peripheral, 360 Hz monitor = 2.8 ms refresh; PC latency and end-to-end System Latency brackets point at NVIDIA FrameView and Reflex Latency Analyzer). Full-width System Score hero at the top, DETECT / AUTOMATE / GUIDE orchestration tile with custom vector illustrations and animated flow arrows between columns. Live GPU sensor dashboard gains 8 snapshot tiles (core clock, memory clock, temp, power, load, fan, PCIe link, throttle status) with severity-graded coloring. PCIe Link OptScore check uses a bandwidth-aware three-tier verdict so intentional x8 setups on Gen4/Gen5 platforms pass without dismissal. New GPU hardware-throttling home banner fires on active HW thermal / power brake / HW slowdown, routes to Cooling. Every OptScore check now has a backing dashboard tile -- six previously-orphaned checks (Fast Startup, Scheduled Tasks bloat, Background Bloat, Monitor Connection, V-Cache Optimizer, GameInput Service) gained tiles; HAGS NavTag corrected to GpuLanding. New section: Analyze > Remnant Detection -- 12-category read-only scan for leftover anti-cheat / security tool / policy tweak artefacts. Welcome tour rebuilt to follow new home layout in reading order. Anti-cheat banner gains inline clickable links to BIOS / BCDEdit / Security pages; header hardware strip segments into three clickable links to each section landing; Gameplay dashboard gains subtitle explaining its consolidation pattern. Honest empty states throughout: CPU clock labelled as Windows' rated figure (overclocks need HWiNFO64 for live readings), mouse polling shows empty state until sampled (auto-retries on first mouse movement), CPU pipeline tile no longer displays ACPI thermal readings because firmware-exposed "CPU" zones on many AM5 boards are board T_Sensor or PCH (not the die), and true die temp requires a kernel driver FAST doesn't ship. UI polish: reveal animations, rainbow A/O/S pill palette, unified elevated tile styling. DisplayPort troubleshooting and dual-PC streaming setup pages added; WinDbg and crash dump guidance generalised.
